rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: Audit Log Retention
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: Audit_Log_Retention.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: Bucket Public Read
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: Bucket_Public_Read.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: Bucket Public Write
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: Bucket_Public_Write.

rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: Group_Has_Too_Many_Admins.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: IAM User API Key Old
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: IAM_User_API_Key_Old.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: IAM User Console Password Old
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: IAM_User_Console_Password_Old.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: Instance Principals Enabled
rules.description: Detects Cloud Guard reporting problem: Instance_Principals_Enabled.

rules.title: Cloud Guard Problem: Policy Too Permissive

rules.title: Linux Archive Data Collected for Exfiltration
rules.description: Detects creation of compressed archives from sensitive directories. Attackers archive collected data (credentials, source code, configs) before exfiltrating to reduce transfer size and avoid detection.

rules.title: Linux At Job Scheduled
rules.description: Detects scheduling of at jobs for deferred command execution. Attackers use at jobs for timed persistence or delayed payload execution.

rules.title: Linux Bind Shell Listener
rules.description: Detects creation of bind shell listeners using netcat, socat, or ncat. Bind shells open a listening port that allows remote attackers to connect and execute commands.

rules.title: Linux Container Escape Attempt
rules.description: Detects patterns indicating container escape attempts including access to Docker socket, cgroup manipulation, or nsenter usage from within containers.

rules.title: Linux Crontab Modification
rules.description: Detects crontab command execution, which may indicate persistence via scheduled tasks.

rules.title: Linux Cryptominer Activity Detected
rules.description: Detects execution of known cryptomining software or command patterns associated with unauthorized cryptocurrency mining on compromised hosts.

rules.title: Linux DNS Tunneling Detected
rules.description: Detects execution of DNS tunneling tools such as iodine, dns2tcp, or dnscat2 used to exfiltrate data or establish covert C2 channels over DNS.

rules.title: Linux Encrypted Channel C2 Communication
rules.description: Detects establishment of encrypted communication channels that may indicate C2 activity. Attackers use OpenSSL, stunnel, and encrypted reverse shells to establish covert command and control over TLS/SSL.

rules.title: Linux Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
rules.description: Detects data exfiltration using non-standard protocols. Attackers use tools like netcat, socat, or raw sockets to send data over TCP/UDP to external hosts, bypassing HTTP/HTTPS-based monitoring.

rules.title: Linux External Remote Service Abuse
rules.description: Detects suspicious use of external remote services (SSH, VPN, RDP via xfreerdp) from unusual or unauthorized sources. Attackers abuse external-facing remote services with stolen or default credentials to gain initial access.

rules.title: Linux Hidden File or Directory Creation in Suspicious Location
rules.description: Detects creation of hidden files or directories (starting with dot) in world-writable or suspicious locations commonly used by attackers to stash tools and payloads.

rules.title: Linux History File Cleared
rules.description: Detects clearing of command history files or unsetting of history environment variable, a common anti-forensics technique.

rules.title: Linux Hosts File Modification
rules.description: Detects modification of /etc/hosts or /etc/resolv.conf. Attackers modify these to redirect traffic, bypass security controls, or perform DNS hijacking.

rules.title: Linux Kernel Module Loaded from Temp Directory
rules.description: Detects loading of kernel modules from temporary directories (/tmp, /dev/shm, /var/tmp), a strong indicator of rootkit installation.

rules.title: Linux LD_PRELOAD Library Hijacking
rules.description: Detects LD_PRELOAD environment variable injection or /etc/ld.so.preload modification, used for library hijacking and process injection.

rules.title: Linux Log File Tampering
rules.description: Detects deletion, truncation, or clearing of system log files. Attackers tamper with logs to cover their tracks and hinder incident response.

rules.title: Linux Network Service Scanning
rules.description: Detects network scanning tools (nmap, masscan, zmap) used for host and service discovery. Attackers use these tools during the discovery phase to map network topology and identify vulnerable services.

rules.title: Linux Password File Direct Modification
rules.description: Detects direct writes to /etc/passwd or /etc/shadow files, bypassing normal user management tools. This could indicate account creation for persistence.

rules.title: Linux Post-Exploitation Enumeration Script
rules.description: Detects execution of common Linux post-exploitation enumeration scripts such as LinPEAS, LinEnum, or linux-exploit-suggester used during privilege escalation.

rules.title: Linux Process Execution from /dev/shm
rules.description: Detects execution of processes from /dev/shm (shared memory filesystem). This in-memory filesystem is frequently used by attackers to stage and execute payloads without touching disk.

rules.title: Linux Process Injection via Ptrace
rules.description: Detects use of ptrace for process injection or debugging, which can be used to inject malicious code into running processes.

rules.title: Linux Process Memory Access via /proc
rules.description: Detects reading process memory via /proc filesystem entries (maps, mem, environ). Used to extract credentials, secrets, or encryption keys from running processes.

rules.title: Linux Proxy and Tunneling Tool Detected
rules.description: Detects proxy and tunneling tools used to create covert communication channels. Attackers use tools like chisel, ligolo, reGeorg, and frp to proxy C2 traffic through compromised hosts and evade network security controls.

rules.title: Linux Reverse Shell Detected
rules.description: Detects common reverse shell patterns in process execution, indicating potential remote access backdoor activity.

rules.title: Linux Sensitive Data Collection from Local System
rules.description: Detects mass access to sensitive system files. Attackers collect data from local systems including SSH keys, cloud credentials, database configs, and application secrets for further exploitation or exfiltration.

rules.title: Linux Setuid Binary Creation
rules.description: Detects creation of setuid/setgid binaries. Attackers set the SUID bit on binaries to maintain root-level access even when running as unprivileged users. This is a classic privilege escalation persistence technique.

rules.title: Linux Shell Profile Persistence
rules.description: Detects modification of shell profile files (.bashrc, .bash_profile, .profile) for persistence. Malicious commands added to these files execute on every user login.

rules.title: Linux SSH Authorized Keys Modified
rules.description: Detects modifications to SSH authorized_keys files, which may indicate backdoor access being established.

rules.title: Linux SSH Failed Login
rules.description: Detects failed SSH login attempts.

rules.title: Linux SSH Tunneling Detected
rules.description: Detects SSH tunneling and port forwarding commands used for pivoting, data exfiltration, or bypassing network security controls.

rules.title: Linux Sudo Usage
rules.description: Detects execution of sudo commands.

rules.title: Linux Sudoers File Modification
rules.description: Detects modifications to the sudoers file or files in sudoers.d directory. Attackers modify sudoers to escalate privileges or maintain persistent elevated access.

rules.title: Linux Suspicious Cron Job Content
rules.description: Detects cron job entries containing suspicious patterns such as downloading payloads, piping to shell, or encoding commands typically used for persistence.

rules.title: Linux Suspicious Download to /tmp
rules.description: Detects file downloads to /tmp directory using common tools. Attackers frequently stage payloads in /tmp as it is world-writable.

rules.title: Linux Suspicious Network Traffic Redirect
rules.description: Detects iptables or nftables modifications that redirect network traffic, which could indicate man-in-the-middle attacks or traffic interception.

rules.title: Linux System Owner and User Discovery
rules.description: Detects commands used to discover system owner, logged-in users, and user context. Attackers run these commands early in post-compromise to understand their privilege level and identify other active users.

rules.title: Linux Systemd Service Persistence
rules.description: Detects creation or enabling of systemd services, a common persistence mechanism where attackers install malicious services that survive reboots.

rules.title: Linux Web Shell File Creation
rules.description: Detects file creation or modification in common web directories that may indicate web shell deployment for persistent remote access.

rules.title: OCI Action: AddUserToGroup
rules.description: Detects the execution of AddUserToGroup action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: AttachInternetGateway

rules.description: Detects the execution of DeleteSubnet action in OCI.

rules.description: Detects the execution of DeleteUser action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: DeleteVcn

rules.title: OCI Action: DetachInternetGateway
rules.description: Detects the execution of DetachInternetGateway action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: RemoveUserFromGroup
rules.description: Detects the execution of RemoveUserFromGroup action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: StartInstance
rules.description: Detects the execution of StartInstance action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: StopInstance
rules.description: Detects the execution of StopInstance action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: TerminateInstance
rules.description: Detects the execution of TerminateInstance action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: UpdateBucket
rules.description: Detects the execution of UpdateBucket action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: UpdatePolicy
rules.description: Detects the execution of UpdatePolicy action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: UpdateRouteTable
rules.description: Detects the execution of UpdateRouteTable action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI Action: UpdateSecurityList
rules.description: Detects the execution of UpdateSecurityList action in OCI.

rules.title: OCI API Key Uploaded
rules.description: Detects upload of a new API key to a user account.

rules.title: OCI Audit Configuration Changed
rules.description: Detects modifications to OCI Audit service configuration including retention period changes. Reducing audit retention is a defense evasion tactic.

rules.title: OCI Auth Token Created
rules.description: Detects creation of auth tokens (SWIFT passwords) which provide programmatic access to OCI Object Storage and Container Registry. Could indicate credential staging for data exfiltration.

rules.title: OCI Autonomous Database Terminated
rules.description: Detects termination of an OCI Autonomous Database, which may indicate sabotage or unauthorized resource destruction.

rules.title: OCI Bastion Session Created
rules.description: Detects creation of a Bastion session providing SSH or port-forwarding access to private resources. Unusual bastion sessions may indicate lateral movement.

rules.title: OCI Cloud Infrastructure Discovery
rules.description: Detects excessive cloud infrastructure enumeration via OCI API calls. Attackers with compromised credentials perform reconnaissance by listing instances, networks, buckets, and databases to map the cloud environment.

rules.title: OCI Cloud Shell Session Started
rules.description: Detects OCI Cloud Shell session activation. Cloud Shell provides authenticated CLI access to the tenancy and can be used for reconnaissance, data access, or lateral movement.

rules.title: OCI Compartment Deleted
rules.description: Detects deletion of an OCI compartment. This is a high-impact destructive action that removes all resources within the compartment and could indicate sabotage or insider threat.

rules.title: OCI Compute Instance Terminated
rules.description: Detects termination of a compute instance.

rules.title: OCI Console Login Failure
rules.description: Detects failed console login attempts to OCI, which may indicate brute-force or credential stuffing attacks.

rules.title: OCI Console Login from Unusual IP
rules.description: Detects a successful login to the OCI Console from an IP address not typically associated with the user.

rules.title: OCI Cross-Region Data Copy
rules.description: Detects cross-region copy operations for boot volumes, block volumes, or object storage buckets. This could indicate data staging for exfiltration to a region outside organizational control.

rules.title: OCI Customer Secret Key Created
rules.description: Detects creation of customer secret keys (S3-compatible access keys) for OCI Object Storage. These keys provide direct API access and may be used for data exfiltration.

rules.title: OCI Database System Terminated
rules.description: Detects termination of OCI Database Systems (BaseDB, Exadata). Unauthorized database termination can result in critical data loss and service disruption.

rules.title: OCI Dynamic Group Created
rules.description: Detects creation of dynamic groups which grant instance principal permissions. Attackers can abuse this for lateral movement from compromised compute instances.

rules.title: OCI Function Invoked
rules.description: Detects invocation of OCI Functions (serverless). Function invocations should be monitored as they can execute arbitrary code with the function's resource principal permissions.

rules.title: OCI IAM Admin Policy Created with Manage All
rules.description: Detects creation of an IAM policy containing overly permissive 'manage all-resources' statement, which grants full administrative access.

rules.title: OCI IAM Policy Modified
rules.description: Detects changes to OCI IAM Policies.

rules.title: OCI Identity Provider Created
rules.description: Detects creation of a new identity provider (SAML/OIDC federation), which could indicate an attacker establishing persistent backdoor access via federated identity.

rules.title: OCI Instance Console Connection Created
rules.description: Detects creation of serial or VNC console connections to compute instances. Console connections bypass network controls and may indicate unauthorized access.

rules.title: OCI KMS Key Scheduled for Deletion
rules.description: Detects scheduling an OCI Vault key for deletion. This can render encrypted data permanently inaccessible.

rules.title: OCI Log Group Deleted
rules.description: Detects deletion of OCI Log Analytics log groups. Deleting log groups destroys collected log data and can indicate an attacker covering their tracks.

rules.title: OCI Network Firewall Policy Modified
rules.description: Detects modifications to OCI Network Firewall policies. Unauthorized changes could weaken network security posture or create bypass routes.

rules.title: OCI Network Load Balancer Deleted
rules.description: Detects deletion of an OCI load balancer, which may cause service outages or indicate infrastructure sabotage.

rules.title: OCI Network Security Group Updated
rules.description: Detects updates to Network Security Groups (NSGs).

rules.title: OCI Notification Subscription Created
rules.description: Detects creation of notification topic subscriptions. Attackers may create subscriptions to exfiltrate alerts or intercept security notifications.

rules.title: OCI Object Storage Bucket Made Public
rules.description: Detects when a bucket's visibility is set to public.

rules.title: OCI Object Storage Pre-Authenticated Request Created
rules.description: Detects creation of pre-authenticated requests (PARs) for OCI Object Storage. PARs bypass IAM authentication and can be used for data exfiltration via shareable URLs.

rules.title: OCI Password Spraying Attack
rules.description: Detects password spraying by monitoring failed OCI Console login attempts across multiple user accounts. Unlike brute force (many attempts against one user), password spraying tries a few passwords against many accounts to avoid lockout thresholds.

rules.title: OCI Route Table Update
rules.description: Detects changes to VCN Route Tables.

rules.title: OCI Security List Allows All Protocols
rules.description: Detects security list updates that allow all protocols (protocol=all) from any source, creating an overly permissive network configuration that violates STIG network segmentation requirements.

rules.title: OCI User MFA Not Enabled
rules.description: Detects OCI user accounts where multi-factor authentication has been disabled or not configured, violating STIG requirement for strong authentication.

rules.title: OCI User Password Reset by Admin
rules.description: Detects an administrator resetting another user's console password. This could indicate account takeover or unauthorized access provisioning.

rules.title: OCI Vault Key Rotation Overdue
rules.description: Detects disabling of key rotation schedule for OCI Vault encryption keys. Key rotation is a STIG requirement for cryptographic key management.

rules.title: OCI Vault Secret Deleted
rules.description: Detects deletion of secrets from OCI Vault. Secret deletion can indicate an attacker covering tracks or disrupting application functionality.

rules.title: OCI VCN Peering Connection Created
rules.description: Detects creation of VCN peering connections (local or remote). Unauthorized peering can extend network access across compartments or regions, enabling lateral movement.

rules.title: OCI VCN Security List Open to World
rules.description: Detects creation or update of a Security List allowing ingress from 0.0.0.0/0.

rules.title: OCI WAF Configuration Updated
rules.description: Detects changes to Web Application Firewall configurations.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of base64
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary base64.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of chmod
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary chmod.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of chown
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary chown.

rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary curl.

rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary dd.

rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary gdb.

rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary passwd.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of perl
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary perl.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of python
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary python.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of rmmod
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary rmmod.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of ruby
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary ruby.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of shadow
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary shadow.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of socat
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary socat.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of strace
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary strace.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of tcpdump
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary tcpdump.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of tshark
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary tshark.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of wget
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary wget.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of whoami
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary whoami.

rules.title: Suspicious Usage of wireshark
rules.description: Detects execution of potentially suspicious binary wireshark.

rules.title: Windows Access Token Manipulation
rules.description: Detects access token manipulation techniques used for privilege escalation. Attackers steal or forge tokens to impersonate other users, especially SYSTEM or domain admin, without needing their credentials.

rules.title: Windows Account Discovery Commands
rules.description: Detects commands used to enumerate user accounts and groups in Active Directory. Attackers perform account discovery to identify privileged accounts, service accounts, and domain administrators for targeting.

rules.title: Windows AMSI Bypass Attempt
rules.description: Detects attempts to bypass the Antimalware Scan Interface (AMSI) through PowerShell commands or .NET reflection techniques.

rules.title: Windows BITS Job Abuse for Persistence
rules.description: Detects abuse of Background Intelligent Transfer Service (BITS) for persistence or payload download. BITS jobs survive reboots and can notify on completion with arbitrary commands.

rules.title: Windows Boot Configuration Modified
rules.description: Detects use of bcdedit.exe to disable recovery mode or modify boot configuration, commonly seen in ransomware attacks to prevent system recovery.

rules.title: Windows Certutil Download or Decode
rules.description: Detects usage of certutil.exe to download files from URLs or decode Base64 payloads, a common LOLBin abuse technique.

rules.title: Windows Credential Dumping via Procdump
rules.description: Detects credential dumping attempts targeting the LSASS process using procdump or similar tools.

rules.title: Windows Data Staging for Exfiltration
rules.description: Detects data staging activity where an attacker collects and stages files before exfiltration. Indicators include copying files to temp directories, creating archives, or writing to removable media staging areas.

rules.title: Windows DLL Side-Loading via Suspicious Path
rules.description: Detects execution of legitimate Windows binaries from non-standard paths, indicating potential DLL side-loading attacks where malicious DLLs are loaded alongside trusted executables.

rules.title: Windows Encoded PowerShell Execution
rules.description: Detects execution of PowerShell with encoded commands, commonly used to obfuscate malicious scripts.

rules.title: Windows Event Log Clearing
rules.description: Detects clearing of Windows event logs via wevtutil or PowerShell. Attackers clear logs to destroy evidence of their activities and hinder forensic investigation.

rules.title: Windows Firewall Rule Modification
rules.description: Detects modification of Windows Firewall rules via netsh. Attackers disable or modify firewall rules to enable lateral movement or C2 communication.

rules.title: Windows Kerberoasting Attack
rules.description: Detects Kerberoasting techniques using tools or PowerShell commands that request TGS tickets for service accounts to crack offline.

rules.title: Windows Keylogger Indicators
rules.description: Detects indicators of keylogging activity. Attackers deploy keyloggers to capture credentials, sensitive data, and user activity. Detects common keylogger tools, SetWindowsHookEx API calls, and suspicious input recording patterns.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: at
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary at.

rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary bitsadmin.

rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary certutil.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: taskkill
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary taskkill.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: tasklist
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary tasklist.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: whoami
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary whoami.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: wmic
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary wmic.

rules.title: Windows LOLBin Usage: wscript
rules.description: Detects usage of Living off the Land binary wscript.

rules.title: Windows LSASS Memory Access
rules.description: Detects tools accessing LSASS process memory for credential dumping. LSASS stores authentication credentials and is a primary target for credential theft.

rules.title: Windows Mimikatz Execution Patterns
rules.description: Detects execution of Mimikatz or tools with Mimikatz-like command line patterns used for credential harvesting from Windows systems.

rules.title: Windows MSBuild Execution for Code Bypass
rules.description: Detects execution of MSBuild.exe which can compile and execute inline C# code from XML project files, bypassing application whitelisting and AppLocker policies.

rules.title: Windows Network Share Discovery
rules.description: Detects commands used to enumerate network shares. Attackers discover shared resources to identify sensitive data repositories, admin shares, and lateral movement paths.

rules.title: Windows NTDS.dit Database Extraction
rules.description: Detects attempts to extract the Active Directory NTDS.dit database file using ntdsutil, vssadmin shadow copies, or direct copy commands. This file contains all domain password hashes.

rules.title: Windows Pass-the-Hash Attack Indicators
rules.description: Detects command-line patterns associated with pass-the-hash attacks using tools like Impacket, CrackMapExec, or built-in Windows commands with NTLM hash parameters.

rules.title: Windows PowerShell Download Cradle
rules.description: Detects PowerShell download cradle patterns commonly used to fetch and execute remote payloads including Invoke-WebRequest, Net.WebClient, and Start-BitsTransfer.

rules.title: Windows Process Hollowing Indicators
rules.description: Detects suspicious process creation patterns indicative of process hollowing where a legitimate process is spawned in a suspended state and its memory replaced with malicious code.

rules.title: Windows PsExec Remote Execution
rules.description: Detects use of PsExec or similar Sysinternals tools for remote command execution, commonly used for lateral movement in compromised networks.

rules.title: Windows RDP Lateral Movement
rules.description: Detects use of mstsc.exe (Remote Desktop client) or enabling RDP via registry, indicating potential lateral movement between systems.

rules.title: Windows Registry Run Key Modification
rules.description: Detects modification of Windows Registry Run/RunOnce keys for persistence, allowing malicious programs to execute at system startup or user login.

rules.title: Windows Remote Access Tool Detected
rules.description: Detects installation or execution of remote access tools commonly abused by attackers. Tools like AnyDesk, TeamViewer, ngrok, and ScreenConnect provide persistent remote access that can bypass firewall rules.

rules.title: Windows Remote System Discovery
rules.description: Detects commands and tools used to discover remote systems on the network. Attackers perform network sweeps, ARP scans, and DNS lookups to identify targets for lateral movement.

rules.title: Windows Scheduled Task Creation via Schtasks
rules.description: Detects creation of scheduled tasks via schtasks.exe with suspicious patterns. Scheduled tasks are a common persistence and execution mechanism.

rules.title: Windows Screen Capture Activity
rules.description: Detects screen capture tools and commands used by attackers to collect visual intelligence from victim systems. Screen captures can reveal sensitive data displayed on screen, credentials in open windows, and security posture.

rules.title: Windows Service Creation via SC
rules.description: Detects creation of new Windows services using sc.exe, which may indicate persistence or privilege escalation.

rules.title: Windows Shadow Copy Deletion
rules.description: Detects deletion of volume shadow copies via vssadmin or wmic, a common ransomware indicator to prevent recovery from encrypted files.

rules.title: Windows Spearphishing Attachment Execution
rules.description: Detects suspicious child processes spawned by Microsoft Office applications, indicating potential macro-based spearphishing payload execution. Office documents should not spawn cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or scripting engines.

rules.title: Windows UAC Bypass Attempt
rules.description: Detects common UAC bypass techniques including fodhelper, eventvwr, and sdclt abuse. These auto-elevate binaries can be hijacked via registry to execute arbitrary code with elevated privileges.

rules.title: Windows WDigest Authentication Enabled for Credential Harvesting
rules.description: Detects enabling WDigest authentication via registry modification. When enabled, plaintext credentials are stored in LSASS memory, making them available for extraction by tools like Mimikatz.

rules.title: Windows WMI Event Subscription Persistence
rules.description: Detects creation of WMI event subscriptions for persistence, allowing attackers to execute code triggered by system events.

hunting_queries.title: DNS Exfiltration Detection (Entropy Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Detects potential DNS tunneling and data exfiltration by identifying DNS queries with unusually long subdomain labels. Long, random-looking subdomains (>30 chars) are indicative of encoded data being exfiltrated via DNS queries. Uses field analysis combined with frequency thresholds.

hunting_queries.title: Linux Data Staging and Exfiltration Indicators
hunting_queries.description: Detects data staging and exfiltration preparation on Linux hosts by identifying hosts where archiving tools (tar, zip, gzip) and network transfer tools (curl, wget, scp, nc) are used in close proximity. Multiple data handling commands on the same host suggest an attacker collecting and exfiltrating data.

hunting_queries.title: Linux Multi-Stage Attack Indicators (Combined Methods)
hunting_queries.description: Detects hosts exhibiting multiple attack stage indicators: initial access (SSH failures), persistence (authorized_keys, crontab), execution (reverse shells, downloads). When 3+ different indicator types appear on the same host, it suggests an active multi-stage attack. Uses combined methods (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods - multi-stage grouping).

hunting_queries.title: Linux Persistence Indicator Score (Combined Methods)
hunting_queries.description: Calculates a persistence risk score per host by counting distinct persistence mechanism indicators (crontab, systemd, authorized_keys, shell profiles, at-jobs, sudoers). A host with multiple persistence vectors indicates thorough attacker entrenchment. Uses combined scoring (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods - risk scoring).

hunting_queries.title: Linux Rare Process Detection (Stacking)
hunting_queries.description: Identifies rarely-seen processes on Linux endpoints. Uses the rare value hunting technique (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking method) to surface processes that appear fewer than 3 times, which may indicate attacker tooling, post-exploitation binaries, or unauthorized software.

hunting_queries.title: C2 Beaconing Detection (Periodic Connection Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Detects command-and-control beaconing by identifying hosts making periodic outbound connections to the same destination. C2 implants typically beacon at regular intervals. Groups network connections by source-destination pair and counts occurrences — a high count to a single destination suggests automated beaconing rather than human browsing.

hunting_queries.title: OCI After-Hours IAM Activity (Time-Based Anomaly)
hunting_queries.description: Detects IAM configuration changes made outside normal business hours (before 06:00 or after 22:00 UTC). Identity changes at unusual hours may indicate a compromised account being used when legitimate users are not monitoring. Uses time-based anomaly detection (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Forecasting/Anomaly Detection - temporal patterns).

hunting_queries.title: OCI Console Login Brute Force (Frequency Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Detects brute force or credential stuffing against OCI Console by counting failed login attempts per user. Uses frequency analysis (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking method) to identify accounts with more than 3 failed attempts in the time window.

hunting_queries.title: OCI IAM Rapid Configuration Changes (Anomaly Detection)
hunting_queries.description: Detects users making a high volume of IAM changes in a short time window. A burst of identity changes (policy updates, user creation, group modifications) from a single principal may indicate account compromise or insider threat. Uses frequency analysis with threshold (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Forecasting/Anomaly Detection method).

hunting_queries.title: OCI Multiple Users from Same IP (Grouping)
hunting_queries.description: Detects a single IP address being used to authenticate as multiple different OCI users. This pattern may indicate credential stuffing, shared compromised infrastructure, or an attacker testing multiple stolen credentials from the same origin. Uses distinct count grouping (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Grouping method).

hunting_queries.title: OCI Privilege Escalation Chain Detection
hunting_queries.description: Detects potential privilege escalation by identifying users who perform a sequence of IAM operations: creating policies, groups, or dynamic groups followed by adding themselves to privileged groups. Combines multiple identity control plane events from the same principal to surface escalation chains.

hunting_queries.title: OCI Resource Destruction Spike (Anomaly Detection)
hunting_queries.description: Identifies principals performing an abnormally high number of destructive operations (delete, terminate). A spike in resource destruction from a single user may indicate a compromised account performing sabotage, ransomware-style destruction, or an insider threat. Uses threshold-based anomaly detection (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Forecasting/Anomaly Detection method).

hunting_queries.title: SSH Brute Force Detection (Frequency Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Identifies SSH brute force attacks by counting failed login attempts per source IP. Uses frequency analysis (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking method) to surface IPs with more than 5 failed attempts, indicating automated credential guessing.

hunting_queries.title: Login Activity Time-Series Anomaly
hunting_queries.description: Detects anomalous spikes in authentication activity using time-bucketed analysis. Groups login events into hourly buckets and flags hours with significantly more activity than the baseline. Uses timecluster-style analysis to identify burst authentication patterns that may indicate credential stuffing or compromised account usage.

hunting_queries.title: Windows Credential Access Tool Cluster (Grouping)
hunting_queries.description: Groups credential access tool indicators by host and user to identify active credential harvesting campaigns. When multiple credential dumping techniques (Mimikatz, LSASS access, Kerberoasting, NTDS.dit) appear on the same host, it indicates systematic credential theft. Uses grouping (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Grouping method).

hunting_queries.title: Windows Defense Evasion Score (Combined Methods)
hunting_queries.description: Calculates a defense evasion risk score per host by counting distinct evasion indicators: event log clearing, AMSI bypass, firewall modification, UAC bypass, process hollowing. Hosts exhibiting multiple evasion techniques indicate sophisticated attackers actively disabling security controls. Uses combined scoring (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Combined Methods - risk scoring).

hunting_queries.title: Windows Lateral Movement Tool Cluster (Grouping)
hunting_queries.description: Groups lateral movement tool usage by host to identify systems being used as pivot points. When multiple lateral movement tools (PsExec, RDP, WinRM, remote shares) appear on the same host, it strongly suggests active lateral movement. Uses grouping/clustering (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Grouping method).

hunting_queries.title: Windows Suspiciously Long Command Line (Field Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Flags processes with unusually long command lines (>500 chars). Attackers frequently use long command lines for encoded payloads, obfuscated scripts, and base64-encoded commands. Uses eval-based field creation (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Searching/Filtering method with field creation).

hunting_queries.title: Windows Process from Unusual Path (Rare Value Analysis)
hunting_queries.description: Identifies processes executing from non-standard paths commonly used by attackers (Temp, Public, AppData, Downloads). Legitimate software typically runs from Program Files or System32. Uses rare path analysis (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking + Field Analysis).

hunting_queries.title: Windows Rare Process Detection (Stacking)
hunting_queries.description: Identifies rarely-seen processes on Windows endpoints. Uses the rare value hunting technique (Threat Hunter's Cookbook: Sorting/Stacking method) to surface processes that appear fewer than 3 times, which may indicate malware, attacker tools, or unauthorized software.
